comments.text: Some computational notes on n(4) (separator-free / property-B encoding) I spent some time attacking the open value n(4) computationally, with help of opus 4.8 and gpt 5.5 thinking, and wanted to share what helped, what didn't, and where the wall is — in case it saves someone else the dead ends, or someone with more compute wants to push further. Code is public: https://github.com/sp4cerat/Wunderlampe-/tree/main/chromatic
After some encounters with GPT 5.3 Thinking and Gemini 3 Pro, the upper bound has been improved to\[n(k) \leq (\frac{e \ln 2}{2}+o(1))k^22^k\]See here

Congrats. Do you mean GPT-5.4 Thinking and Gemini 3.1 Pro, or are you really using old models? Standard check finds no issues. However, it seems this result is not new. Erdos in 1964 proved that $m(k) \le (e (\log 2)/4 + o(1) ) k^2 2^k$, and we also know that $n(k) \le 2 m(k)$. The result is a combination of these results. This is found by GPT-5.4 Thinking during the check.

It seems that a sharper lower bound for $n(k)$ is available. By the results in [ERT80] (see also [HMT96, Section 1]), we know that $m(k)\le n(k)\le 2m(k)$, where $m(k)$ denote the minimum number of edges possible in a 3-chromatic $k$-uniform hypergraph. With the current best lower bound $m(k)=\Omega \left(2^k\sqrt{k/\log k}\right)$ due to Radhakrishnan and Srinivasan [RaSr20] (see also [GLW24]), we obtain\[ n(k)=\Omega\left(2^k\sqrt{\frac{k}{\log k}}\right). \]References. [GLW24] Grill, Karl, Daniel Linzmayer, and T. U. Wien. "Improved Lower Bounds for Property B." arXiv:2403.05674v3. [RaSr20] Radhakrishnan, Jaikumar, and Aravind Srinivasan. "Improved bounds and algorithms for hypergraph 2-coloring." Random Structures & Algorithms 16.1 (2000): 4-32. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
